_note: Starter mnemonics for Japanese vocab. Keyed by entry text. Never overwrites user-written mnemonics.
こんにちは: Break it down: kon-ni-chi-wa. 'This day' (今日) + wa (は). Literally greeting the current day — 'as for today!' Think: 'Come, need cheap water?' as a silly hook.
ありがとう: Sounds like 'ah-ree-ga-toh'. Imagine saying 'Ah, Lee got oh-so many thanks!' A grateful sigh: 'Ahh, ree-ga-toh!'
はい: hai = yes. Short, sharp, like a soldier snapping 'Hai!' in affirmation. One syllable, instant yes.
いいえ: i-i-e = no. Three syllables of hesitation — 'ee-ee-eh, no no no.' The double い softens the refusal.
りんご: ringo = apple. Sounds like 'ring-go' — imagine a ringing bell shaped like an apple 🍎🔔.
みず: mizu = water. 'Me-zoo' — imagine you at a zoo, desperately needing water in the heat.
ほん: hon = book. Sounds like 'hone' — you hone your skills by reading books 📚.
ねこ: neko = cat. 'Neh-ko' sounds like 'neck-oh' — cats always rub their necks on things.
いぬ: inu = dog. 'Ee-noo' — imagine a dog saying 'I knew it!' when you come home.
たべる: taberu = to eat. 'Tah-beh-roo' — imagine a table (ta-be) full of food you're about to eat.
のむ: nomu = to drink. 'No-moo' — imagine saying 'No moo! Give me water, not milk!'
おおきい: ookii = big. The double お and double い stretch the word out — it's a BIG long word for big things.
ちいさい: chiisai = small. 'Chee-sai' — a tiny cheese (chii) that's small (sai-ze). Mini cheese = small.
いい: ii = good. The simplest word — just 'ee ee', like nodding yes twice with a smile.
ひと: hito = person. 'Hee-toh' — a person saying 'heat-oh, it's warm!' The kanji 人 looks like a walking person.
ともだち: tomodachi = friend. 'Toh-mo-da-chi' — 'Tomorrow, da chi (let's) meet!' Friends make plans for tomorrow.
がくせい: gakusei = student. 'Gah-koo-say' — imagine a student saying 'Gack! You say I have homework?'
せんせい: sensei = teacher. Famous from karate/anime — the wise master. せん (before) + せい (life) = one who came before.
はたらく: hataraku = to work. 'Hah-ta-ra-koo' — 'Hut! Ta-ra-koo!' like a work chant keeping rhythm.
べんきょうする: benkyou suru = to study. 'Ben-kyoh' — imagine studying hard until you're 'bent' from leaning over books.
すき: suki = to like. 'Ski' — you like skiing! Whatever you love, imagine doing it on skis ⛷️.
きれい: kirei = beautiful/clean. 'Kee-ray' — like a beautiful ray of light (ray = kirei). Clean, bright, beautiful.
うれしい: ureshii = happy. 'Oo-reh-shee' — 'Ooh, refreshing!' That feeling of joy when something refreshes you.
はやい: hayai = fast/early. 'Hah-yai' — 'Hi-ya!' like a karate chop — fast and decisive.
おそい: osoi = slow/late. 'Oh-so-ee' — 'Oh, so easy' (taking it slow). Slow and leisurely.
かう: kau = to buy. 'Cow' — you buy a cow at the market 🐄. kau = cow = purchase.
おかね: okane = money. 'Oh-ka-neh' — 'Oh! Ka-ching!' The sound of money. お is an honorific — money is respected in Japanese.
医者: isha = doctor. 医 (i) = medicine; 者 (sha) = person. A medicine-person. 'Ee-sha' — sounds like 'I shall heal you.'
経済: keizai = economy. 'Kay-zai' — 'Kay, sigh' — the economy makes everyone sigh. 経 = pass through, 済 = benefit.
けれども: keredomo = although/but. 'Keh-reh-do-mo' — 'Care to do more? But...' A gentle contradiction.
わかる: wakaru = to understand. 'Wah-ka-roo' — 'Waka roo!' like a kangaroo hopping to understanding 🦘.
言語: gengo = language. 言 (gen) = speech/word; 語 (go) = language. Words + language = language itself.
旅行する: ryokou suru = to travel. 旅 (ryo) = journey; 行 (kou) = go. 'Ryo-koh' — 'Rio? Go!' — travel to exciting places.
自由: jiyuu = freedom. 自 (ji) = self; 由 (yuu) = reason/from. Freedom = acting from your own reasons. 'Jee-yoo' = free you.
決める: kimeru = to decide. 'Kee-meh-roo' — 'Key, me rule' — you hold the key, you make the ruling decision.
